|  | 1.8 | 50.80526343 | 45.72473708 | 41.15226337 | 37.03703704 | 33.33333333 | 30 | 27.27272727 | 24.79338843 | 22.53944403 | 20.49040366 | 18.62763969 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | The table above shows what your CPC (cost per click) and conversion rate (based on your goal) need to be in order to remain under your target acquisition cost. The green areas are below your cost per acquisition target and the red would bring you above. 

In this example above if your CPC remained at 1.2 (in your local currency) to achieve your CPA target of 20 would not work with a 4% conversion rate. The green areas shows CPA below target, so a conversion rate of 4.37% and CPC of 0.72 would bring your CPA in at 16.46, below your target.

Adjust your values in the top area to model your scenarios which you could, for example, copy and paste into a document for a client or use in your own projections. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  | 24 | 1.59924375 | 1.7769375 | 1.974375 | 2.19375 | 2.4375 | 2.708333333 | 2.979166667 | 3.277083333 | 3.604791667 | 3.965270833 | 4.361797917 |
|  | 26 | 1.476225 | 1.64025 | 1.8225 | 2.025 | 2.25 | 2.5 | 2.75 | 3.025 | 3.3275 | 3.66025 | 4.026275 |
|  | 28 | 1.370780357 | 1.523089286 | 1.692321429 | 1.880357143 | 2.089285714 | 2.321428571 | 2.553571429 | 2.808928571 | 3.089821429 | 3.398803571 | 3.738683929 |
|  | 30 | 1.279395 | 1.42155 | 1.5795 | 1.755 | 1.95 | 2.166666667 | 2.383333333 | 2.621666667 | 2.883833333 | 3.172216667 | 3.489438333 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | The table above shows what your CPA (cost per acquisition) and AOV (average order value) need to be in order to remain over your ROAS (return on ad spend). The green areas are above your ROAS target and the red would bring you below. 

In this example above if your CPA remained at 20 (in your local currency) to achieve your ROAS target of 3 would not work with a 40 AOV. The green areas shows ROAS above target, so an AOV 32.40 and CPA of 10 would bring your ROAS in at 3.24, above your target.This is of course difficult so you either need to shift AOV higher or reduce your ROAS goal if CPA is not achievable

Adjust your values in the top area to model your scenarios which you could, for example, copy and paste into a document for a client. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| WHAT IF CALCULATOR FOR LTV |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| To use this calculator, enter your current repeat purchase rate and AOV (for both new and return customers) plus Goal LTV (without currencies) in the yellow cells only, then edit the numbers to get the grid into the green based on your acquisition goal cost. Red cells mean you're off target. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| To edit this document, make a local copy first by clicking File > Make a Copy. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| What is your AOV (average order value)? |  |  |  |  | 65 |  < enter your average AOV here (new and repeat customers) |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| What is your target LTV? |  |  |  |  | 100 |  < enter your target LTV here |  |  |  | www.BPMmethod.com  |  |  |
| Calculated LTV: |  |  |  |  | 78 | based on your current repeat rate and AOV |  |  |  | Join The Facebook Experts Group |  |  |
| (do not edit the below table) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  |  | 38.38185 | 42.6465 | 47.385 | 52.65 | 58.5 | 65 | 71.5 | 78.65 | 86.515 | 95.1665 | 104.68315 |
| PUR Rate | 0.6 | 23.02911 | 25.5879 | 28.431 | 31.59 | 35.1 | 39 | 42.9 | 47.19 | 51.909 | 57.0999 | 62.80989 |
|  | 0.72 | 27.634932 | 30.70548 | 34.1172 | 37.908 | 42.12 | 46.8 | 51.48 | 56.628 | 62.2908 | 68.51988 | 75.371868 |
|  | 0.84 | 32.240754 | 35.82306 | 39.8034 | 44.226 | 49.14 | 54.6 | 60.06 | 66.066 | 72.6726 | 79.93986 | 87.933846 |
|  | 0.96 | 36.846576 | 40.94064 | 45.4896 | 50.544 | 56.16 | 62.4 | 68.64 | 75.504 | 83.0544 | 91.35984 | 100.495824 |
|  | 1.08 | 41.452398 | 46.05822 | 51.1758 | 56.862 | 63.18 | 70.2 | 77.22 | 84.942 | 93.4362 | 102.77982 | 113.057802 |
|  | 1.2 | 46.05822 | 51.1758 | 56.862 | 63.18 | 70.2 | 78 | 85.8 | 94.38 | 103.818 | 114.1998 | 125.61978 |
|  | 1.32 | 50.664042 | 56.29338 | 62.5482 | 69.498 | 77.22 | 85.8 | 94.38 | 103.818 | 114.1998 | 125.61978 | 138.181758 |
|  | 1.44 | 55.269864 | 61.41096 | 68.2344 | 75.816 | 84.24 | 93.6 | 102.96 | 113.256 | 124.5816 | 137.03976 | 150.743736 |
|  | 1.56 | 59.875686 | 66.52854 | 73.9206 | 82.134 | 91.26 | 101.4 | 111.54 | 122.694 | 134.9634 | 148.45974 | 163.305714 |
|  | 1.68 | 64.481508 | 71.64612 | 79.6068 | 88.452 | 98.28 | 109.2 | 120.12 | 132.132 | 145.3452 | 159.87972 | 175.867692 |
|  | 1.8 | 69.08733 | 76.7637 | 85.293 | 94.77 | 105.3 | 117 | 128.7 | 141.57 | 155.727 | 171.2997 | 188.42967 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | The table above shows what your repeat purchase rate and AOV (average order value) need to be in order to remain over your LTV (Lifetime Value) goal. The green areas are above your LTV target and the red would bring you below. 

In this example above if your repeat rate remained at 1.2, then you would NOT reach your 100 LTV target - you'd end up with 78. The green areas shows LTV above target, so an AOV of 65 and repeat rate of 1.56 would bring your LTV in at 101.40, above your 100 target. There is always a balance between increasing the number of new customers and getting existing customers to buy more.

Adjust your values in the top area to model your scenarios which you could, for example, copy and paste into a document for a client or for your own business planning. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
